meta_title: For Freelancers: project management to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Project Management to Solve Debugging Errors
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with unfixable bugs. Learn how to leverage project management workflows with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship stable SaaS products faster.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about code; it's about architectural visibility. By integrating structured project management with Claude Code’s contextual awareness and Antigravity’s state tracking, you isolate failures, document dependencies, and resolve complex logic errors in minutes rather than hours, ensuring your production deployments remain rock-solid and client-ready.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is pinging you on Slack, and you’re staring at a stack trace that makes absolutely no sense. You’ve refactored, you’ve commented out blocks, and yet the bug remains—an invisible ghost in the machine that’s eating your profit margins and your sanity. We’ve all been there, trapped in a loop of 'fix one, break two.'

But the secret to elite freelance output isn't being a better debugger; it’s being a better project manager. When you treat your codebase like a managed product rather than a chaotic canvas, debugging becomes a predictable, repeatable process. In this guide, we’ll wire up your workflow with Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your debugging nightmares into systematic wins.

steps.title: Define the Failure State
steps.description: Before touching the code, document the exact failure path. Use Antigravity to generate a formal bug report that isolates the environment, inputs, and expected outputs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and create a structured bug report template that captures the current state, potential side effects, and the exact sequence of events leading to the failure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Generate a reproduction script for the current state defined in the Antigravity report.'

steps.title: Map Dependency Chains
steps.description: Map the relationship between your modules. Understanding the 'blast radius' of a change prevents accidental regressions during the debugging process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a dependency map for the affected module and list all downstream functions that could be impacted by a fix here.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'List all files that import the problematic function and verify their current export structure.'

steps.title: Automated Isolation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to wrap the problematic logic in a sandboxed test harness, allowing you to iterate on the fix without risking the production build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust test harness for the isolated module that mocks external API calls and validates the core logic against the failure state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Run the test harness and identify the exact line where the assertion fails.'

steps.title: Iterative Patching
steps.description: Apply your fix using an incremental approach. Validate each change against your test harness before merging back into the main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the logic to handle the identified edge case while maintaining compatibility with the existing interface.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Apply the suggested patch and run the test harness to ensure zero regressions.'

steps.title: Documentation for Future-Proofing
steps.description: Update your project management documentation to prevent this specific class of bug from recurring, turning the fix into a permanent architectural guardrail.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Update the project README and internal documentation to include this new architectural constraint and the rationale behind the fix.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Commit the changes with a detailed message referencing the original bug report ID.'

pro_tips: Use Claude Code's 'history' flag to revert to a 'last known stable state' instantly when a refactor goes off the rails.
Never debug in production; always use Antigravity to spin up a ephemeral dev environment that mirrors your live database schema.
Treat every bug as a missing unit test; if you didn't write a test for it, you haven't actually fixed it.
Automate your commit messages with Claude Code to include the Jira/Linear ticket ID, ensuring your project management board stays perfectly synced.
Use Antigravity to periodically audit your codebase for 'technical debt hotspots' before they turn into production-level bugs.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully patched the bug, integrate these steps into your CI/CD pipeline to ensure that your project management documentation is automatically updated whenever the code changes.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start commanding it. With these workflows, you'll reduce your debugging time by 70% and regain your reputation as a high-velocity, precision-focused freelancer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
